one.heading: Go beyond one-size-fits-all
one.description: Choose from leading LLMs optimized for speed, accuracy, or cost.

two.heading: Use your agents, your way
two.description: Use GitHub Copilot, your own custom agents, or the third-party ones you already rely on.

three.description: Copilot works where you do: in GitHub, your IDE, project tools, chat apps, and custom MCP servers.

workflow.label: Workflow
workflow.heading: Code, command, and collaborate
workflow.description: AI that works where you do, whether in your editor, on the command line, or across GitHub.

one.heading: Make your editor your most powerful accelerator
one.description: Copilot in your editor does it all, from explaining concepts and completing code, to proposing edits and validating files with agent mode.
one.imageAlt: GitHub Copilot agent mode in VS Code

two.heading: Ship faster with AI that codes with you
two.description: Assign issues directly to Copilot and let it autonomously write code, create pull requests, and respond to feedback in the background.
two.imageAlt: GitHub Copilot coding agent working on a GitHub issue

three.heading: Bring AI to your terminal workflow
three.description: Direct Copilot in the terminal using natural language and watch it plan, build, and execute complex workflows powered by your GitHub context.
three.imageAlt: GitHub Copilot CLI working in the terminal

bento.heading: Grupo Boticario increases developer productivity by 94% with Copilot.
bento.imageAlt: Grupo Boticario team members collaborating in an office setting

organization.heading: Tailor-made for your organization
organization.description: Shape Copilot to your business needs. Customize what it knows, how it acts, and where it connects.

one.heading: Turn Copilot into a project expert
one.description: Scale knowledge and keep teams consistent by creating a shared source of truth that includes context from your docs and repositories.

two.heading: Manage agent usage with enterprise-grade controls
two.description: Track activity with detailed audit logs and enforce governance by managing agents from a single control plane.

three.heading: Secure your MCP integrations
three.description: Control which MCP servers developers can access from their IDEs, and use allow lists to prevent unauthorized access.

free.heading: Free
free.description: A fast way to get started with GitHub Copilot.

features.one: 50 agent mode or chat requests per month
features.three: Access to Haiku 4.5, GPT-4.1, and more

pro.label: Most popular

features.two: Unlimited agent mode and chats with GPT-5 mini

pro.footnote: Free for verified students, teachers, and maintainers of popular open source projects.

proPlus.heading: Pro+
proPlus.description: Scale with agents and more models.

features.one: Access to all models, including Claude Opus 4.1 and more
features.two: 5x more premium requests than Pro to use the latest models, with the option to buy more

one.question: What is GitHub Copilot?
one.answer: GitHub Copilot is GitHub's AI developer toolset for code completion, chat, agent workflows, and assistance across the software development lifecycle.

one.question: What are the differences between the Free, Pro, Business, and Enterprise plans?
one.answer: Free is a limited entry point for trying Copilot. Pro adds coding agent access, broader model choice, and premium requests for individual developers. Business and Enterprise add organization-wide controls, governance, and admin capabilities for teams operating at scale.

two.question: How can I upgrade my GitHub Copilot Free license to Copilot Pro?
two.answer: You can upgrade from your GitHub account billing settings by choosing Copilot Pro or Pro+. That keeps your Copilot setup intact while expanding the models, requests, and features available to you.

three.question: What is included in GitHub Copilot Free?
three.answer: GitHub Copilot Free includes 50 agent mode or chat requests per month, 2,000 code completions per month, and access to a starter set of supported models.

four.question: Which plan includes GitHub Copilot Autofix?
four.answer: Autofix availability depends on the GitHub plan and security products attached to your organization. Use the compare page or plan documentation for the latest eligibility details.

one.question: How does GitHub Copilot handle my code and data?
one.answer: GitHub publishes plan-specific privacy details so teams can understand how prompts, suggestions, and related telemetry are handled for their chosen deployment model.

one.question: How does GitHub approach responsible AI?
one.answer: GitHub documents its approach to responsible AI through model policies, safeguards, transparency guidance, and product controls designed to support safe adoption.
